feed_role: Lab = generator/feed. The clinic SM pipeline (cockpit) owns GATE-1 dedupe, floor-locked rendering, the single preview, and publishing.
note: One creative reused across Instagram, Facebook & Google Business Profile. 10 posts to choose from, diversified by format/hook/topic, each modelled on a niche over-performer and rewritten in EHN clinician voice. Each post carries a routing block mapping it to the pipeline's pillar→format schedule.

pillar_format_map.conversion · Sun: text-led CTA carousel (booking/assessment link in the caption, not on a card)

palette.note: EHN palette only — never the competitor colours/fonts.

typography.note: NOT Libre Caslon — that is the branded-card lock, benched for this experiment.

render_constraints.rule: Shorten copy, never shrink font. The renderer hard-asserts >=36px and crashes the build on an under-floor size.
render_constraints.card_branding: Cards carry the business name "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green close line) — NOT the suburb. Suburb keyword lives in the GBP caption only (local SEO).
render_constraints.gbp: Every post ships a GBP hook card, including text-only days (Zernio silently schedules a card-less GBP post — always supply one).

posts.visual_recipe: Solid muted sage-green background with centered, large serif headline in white italics ("Why Calcium-D-Glucarate is a Game-Changer for Histamine Intolerance"), smaller supporting body text below in white sans-serif, small uppercase label at top ("CALCIUM-D-GLUCARATE"), slide counter badge (1/7) in top right, footer handle in small caps. Minimal, high-contrast, professional wellness aesthetic.
posts.angle: Oestrogen dominance fuels histamine release—here's the hidden loop you need to know about.
posts.build_brief:
Format: 7-slide carousel (4:4 Instagram, 1080×1350px per slide).

Canvas & background: Each slide has a clean, warm cream background (#F5F1ED or soft off-white) with EHN green #39B54A as the primary visual anchor. No full-background colour wash—keep slides bright and open.

Headline treatment: Large, bold Satoshi headline (95–110px relative scale on 1080px canvas) in charcoal (#2C2C2C), centered, with ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic (e.g. "Why Oestrogen Dominance *Stokes* Histamine Release"). Keep headline to 1–2 lines; place it in the top-to-mid zone with generous whitespace above and below.

Supporting copy: Smaller Satoshi Medium body text (relative 40px+ equivalent on 1080 canvas) in charcoal, centered, 1–3 short sentences per slide. No more than 5–6 words per line; prioritise clarity and breath over density.

Visual anchor: Each slide features a small, minimal icon or soft illustration in green #39B54A (e.g. a simple molecule outline, a subtle wave pattern, a dot connector) positioned left or right of text, never competing for centre attention. Keep the icon editorial, grounded, never clinical-cartoon.

Slide structure (faithfully replicating winner's whitespace/density):
- Slide 1 (hook): Small uppercase label top-left in grey ("OESTROGEN & HISTAMINE") | Large headline centred | Minimal green icon below | Slide counter "1/7" top right, small caps.
- Slides 2–6 (loop/explainer): Same header/counter treatment | Headline + 1–2 supporting lines | Icon anchors visually without cluttering.
- Slide 7 (close): Larger body text (relative 45px+) in charcoal, centred, with green #39B54A close line "Elemental Health & Nutrition" at bottom in bold Satoshi Medium. Small logo lockup (unobtrusive) next to or below brand name.

Colour harmony: Cream background + charcoal text + green #39B54A icons/accent word creates high contrast and clinical warmth. No secondary colours that clash—keep the palette minimal and intentional. The green must *feel* like the hero.

Density & whitespace: Match the winner's minimal, open feel—25–35% of slide is active content (text + icon), 65–75% breathing room. Never pack slides; silence is strength.

Typography lock: Satoshi Bold (700) for all headlines; Satoshi Medium (500) for body. No serif, no italics on body—only the one green-accent word in the headline gets italics.

Never below 36px on any text; body floors at 40px+ equivalent. Shorten copy rather than shrink font.
posts.design_recipe:
Reusable myth-bust carousel shell for EHN functional-medicine topics.

Format: 7-slide carousel (4:4 Instagram, 1080×1350px per slide).

Canvas & background: Warm cream off-white (#F5F1ED) across all slides. EHN green #39B54A is the sole visual accent and primary anchor—appears in icons, one italic word per headline, and the close line.

Headline treatment: Large, bold Satoshi headline (95–110px relative scale) in charcoal (#2C2C2C), centred, with ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic. Headline is 1–2 lines max. Generous whitespace above and below.

Body copy: Satoshi Medium (40px+ equivalent), charcoal, centred, max 3 lines per slide. 5–6 words per line; prioritise breath and legibility.

Visual anchor: Small, minimal icon in green #39B54A (molecule, wave, connector, or topic-relevant shape) positioned left or right of centre text, never cluttering. Editorial, grounded style—no cartoon or stock imagery.

Slide structure (reusable across all topics):
- Slide 1: Small uppercase label top-left in grey ("TOPIC LABEL") | [headline] centred | [icon] below | Slide counter "1/7" top right in small caps.
- Slides 2–6: Same header/counter layout | [headline] | [supporting line 1] | [supporting line 2] | [icon].
- Slide 7: Larger body text (45px+ equivalent) centred | Green #39B54A close line "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in bold Satoshi Medium | Small logo lockup below or adjacent.

Colour harmony: Cream + charcoal + green #39B54A only. No secondary accent colours; green is the sole hero. High contrast, minimal, clinical-warm.

Whitespace: 25–35% active content (text + icon), 65–75% breathing room. Silence is strength; never pack slides.

Typography lock: Satoshi Bold (700) headlines, Satoshi Medium (500) body. One green italic word per headline; no italics on body. Never below 36px on any text; body floors at 40px+.

Legibility rule: SHORTEN COPY, NEVER SHRINK THE FONT. If copy is long, trim it. Card branding: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A, bottom of Slide 7.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1: OESTROGEN & HISTAMINE | Why Oestrogen Dominance *Stokes* Histamine Release | [green molecule icon]

Slide 2: The Loop Explained | Excess oestrogen triggers mast cells | They release histamine

Slide 3: What Happens Next | More histamine means less DAO | Your natural histamine brake fails

Slide 4: The Perimenopause Connection | Fluctuating oestrogen = mast cell chaos | Classic histamine flare signs appear

Slide 5: What You Might Feel | Brain fog, bloating, joint ache | Anxiety, sleep disruption, flushed skin

Slide 6: The Functional Path | Stabilise oestrogen metabolism first | Support mast cell calm + DAO

Slide 7: Not a quick fix—but worth understanding. If oestrogen dominance fuels your histamine load, we work backwards to root cause. Book a consultation to map your hormonal and histamine picture. | Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Oestrogen dominance and histamine intolerance aren't separate problems—they're linked by a hidden loop.

When oestrogen is high, mast cells release more histamine. At the same time, excess oestrogen can suppress DAO (the enzyme that breaks histamine down). The result? Your body gets flooded and can't clear it.

This loop is especially common in perimenopause, when oestrogen swings wildly.

The good news: we can stabilise oestrogen metabolism and calm mast cell reactivity at the same time. It takes patience and the right investigation—but it works.

If you're experiencing brain fog, bloating, anxiety, or joint ache alongside your cycle, reach out. We'd love to map your hormonal picture and find the root cause.

Book a consultation—let's work backwards together.

#HistamineIntolerance #OestrogenDominance #Perimenopause #FunctionalMedicine #HormoneBalance
posts.gmb_caption: Oestrogen dominance fuels histamine release—a hidden loop many miss in perimenopause. We help you stabilise hormonal metabolism and calm mast cell reactivity at the root. Book an appointment in Eastwood, Adelaide today to map your hormonal picture.

posts.visual_recipe: Talking-head portrait shot of a woman with long dark hair, warm neutral background (home office setting), filmed from shoulders-up with authentic conversational expression. Teal/turquoise rounded rectangle text overlay at bottom with white bold sans-serif text reading 'The secret to getting kids to open up' (8 words). Light, approachable setting with minimal branding elements visible in soft-focus background.
posts.angle: Walking as a conversation bridge — unlocking emotional connection and nervous-system calm in one simple habit.
posts.build_brief: Format: Reel/video (shoulders-up talking-head portrait, woman or practitioner, authentic conversational expression, home clinic or warm neutral background, soft-focus and minimal branding). Hook frame on-screen text: EHN green #39B54A rounded rectangle at bottom with Satoshi Bold white text (headline ~95px scale on 1080 canvas, ~8 words max). Background: warm cream or soft white, never pure black or the competitor's teal. Primary visual anchor: EHN green #39B54A in the text overlay rectangle (this is the colour that lands first and anchors the whole frame). Secondary palette: if a secondary shape or accent appears, choose soft sage, muted grey-blue, or warm terracotta — colours that harmonize with green, never clash. Layout structure (COPY FAITHFULLY): shoulders-up framing, warm home-clinic setting, natural light, minimal clutter in background, talking-head authenticity, single high-contrast text overlay at lower third (rounded rectangle, EHN green border or fill), white Satoshi Bold headline text centred inside the shape. Whitespace: generous around the text shape, breathing room to the edges of frame. Legibility: headline bold and large (~95px scale), body copy in the script/captions below (40px minimum, Satoshi Medium, charcoal or warm neutral). Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in EHN green or complementary neutral, small and unobtrusive. Feel: clinical warmth, editorial grounding, Australian natural tones, no stock-wellness clichés. No logo bloom or branding clutter — let the green anchor and the practitioner's presence speak.
posts.design_recipe: Format: Reel/video talking-head portrait (shoulders-up, authentic conversational expression, warm neutral home/clinic background, soft-focus minimal branding). Hook text overlay: EHN green #39B54A rounded rectangle positioned at lower third, white Satoshi Bold text (headline scale ~95px on 1080, ~8 words max, centred). Primary visual anchor: green #39B54A in the overlay shape — this is the dominant colour that lands first. Secondary palette (if accent shapes/borders needed): soft sage, muted grey-blue, or warm terracotta — harmonize with green, never clash with it. Background: warm cream, soft white, or muted neutral (never pure black or competitor's exact palette). Typography: Satoshi Bold for headline (700 weight), Satoshi Medium for body/script captions (500 weight, 40px minimum). Layout structure: generous whitespace around text overlay, breathing room to frame edges, talking-head framing centred, single high-contrast overlay at lower third. Text hierarchy: hook headline bold and prominent (~95px scale), supporting line or script below in body (40px+, charcoal or warm neutral). Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" small and unobtrusive (green or complementary neutral). Feel: clinical warmth, editorial grounding, natural tones, no stock-wellness aesthetic. Legibility floor (hard): all on-frame text >=36px, body >=40px, headline ~95px+ on 1080 canvas — shorten copy, never shrink font. No cure/treat/guarantee claims. Small logo lockup and CTA (booking destination) soft-placed, non-intrusive.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1 (Hook frame, on-screen overlay):
Headline (in EHN green #39B54A rounded rectangle, Satoshi Bold, white text, ~95px scale):
"The real gift of a walk"

Supporting line (below, Satoshi Medium, charcoal, body text ~40px+):
Connection over steps.

Close line (small, Satoshi Bold, EHN green or warm neutral):
Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Walking does something remarkable. It's not just about the steps — it's about what happens when you show up with someone else. A walk together creates a little island of time where conversation can breathe, where bodies move gently, and where nervous systems settle.

Whether it's a partner, a friend, a kid, or someone you haven't connected with in a while, walking side-by-side opens a door. There's less eye contact pressure, there's movement, and there's permission to think together rather than *at* each other.

If you're looking to deepen a relationship — or just need a moment of calm — take someone on a walk this week. Notice what happens when you both slow down.

Who will you walk with? Let me know in the comments.

#Walking #FunctionalHealth #MindBodyConnection #ElementalHealth #Wellness
posts.gmb_caption: Walking is one of the most underrated tools for emotional and nervous-system health. When you walk with someone, you create space for real connection. If you'd like to talk about how movement fits into your overall wellness plan, book an appointment with our team in Adelaide.

posts.visual_recipe: Dark teal/charcoal solid background with bold white sans-serif headline ("HOW I REVERSED MY BIOLOGICAL AGE") occupying upper 40% of frame in heavy weight, followed by red accent text emphasizing the key metric ("FROM 52 TO 42"). Lower 60% features a professional close-up portrait of a middle-aged man wearing glasses in a home office setting (shelves visible, warm lighting), holding what appears to be supplement bottles, establishing credibility and tangibility for the health claim.
posts.angle: How unmasking a hidden methylation block changed one client's energy and longevity outlook—without fancy supplements.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: single square card (1080 × 1080px, 16:9 optimised for feed). Background: warm cream (#F5F1ED) to harmonize with EHN green and create clinical warmth. Layout mirrors winner exactly: bold headline occupies upper 40% of frame (no more), followed by a green-accented metric line in the middle, then lower 60% holds a grounded lifestyle photograph.

Headline: "YOUR BODY'S HIDDEN SWITCH" positioned top-left, set in Satoshi Bold ~95px, charcoal (#2D2D2D), with ONE meaningful word ("SWITCH") rendered in EHN green #39B54A italic to anchor the visual.

Metric accent line: "methylation matters" in Satoshi Bold ~52px, green #39B54A, positioned centre–right, creating a secondary visual anchor that echoes the winner's red-accent structure.

Lower 60% image area: A grounded editorial photograph of an adult (30–50s, any gender) in a neutral interior (home study, clinic waiting room, kitchen counter with morning light), holding a simple lab report or wellness journal, natural window light, warm tones. Photograph should feel authentic, not stock—worn-in textures, real light, real hands. No supplement bottles visible; keep it clinical-grounded, not commercial.

Whitespace: ample breathing room around headline and metric line (12–15% margins). Image sits flush to bottom and sides, creating a clean split between text and visual that mirrors the winner's density.

Accent colour: EHN green #39B54A used sparingly in the metric line and one italicised word to anchor the brand without overwhelming. No secondary accent needed; green + cream + charcoal = complete palette.

Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium ~28px, charcoal, bottom-right corner, with a small green underline (2–3px).

Type lock: Satoshi Bold (headline), Satoshi Medium (body, metric, close line). Never Libre Caslon.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: single square card (1080 × 1080px, optimised for feed). Background: warm cream (#F5F1ED) to harmonize with EHN green #39B54A, creating a clinical-warm foundation.

Layout (reusable template): 
— Upper 40%: headline space. Satoshi Bold, ~95px, charcoal, with ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic to anchor the visual.
— Centre band (15–20% height): metric or key insight line in Satoshi Bold ~52px, EHN green #39B54A, positioned centre–right or left (alternate per topic for rhythm).
— Lower 60%: grounded editorial photograph (portrait or lifestyle, real lighting, authentic moment, no clichés). Image sits flush to bottom/sides; creates clean text–image split.

Whitespace: 12–15% margins around headline and metric line; generous breathing room to avoid visual clutter.

Accent palette: EHN green #39B54A as primary anchor (headline word + metric line). Secondary colours: cream background (#F5F1ED), charcoal text (#2D2D2D) for legibility. No clashing accent needed; green + cream + charcoal = sufficient and intentional.

Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium ~28px, charcoal, bottom corner, with subtle 2–3px green underline.

Type: Satoshi Bold (headline), Satoshi Medium (metric, body, close). Legibility floor: headline ≥95px, body ≥40px, all text ≥36px on 1080 canvas. SHORTEN COPY TO FIT; never shrink font below legibility floor.

Feel: clinical-grounded, editorial, warm. Photography is real (natural light, lived-in spaces, authentic subjects), never stock-wellness or overcleaned. Green anchor ensures EHN brand feels present without visual overwhelm.
posts.on_image:
Headline: "YOUR BODY'S HIDDEN SWITCH" (with "SWITCH" in green #39B54A italic)
Metric line: "methylation matters"
[Lower 60%: grounded lifestyle photograph—adult with lab report or wellness journal, real natural light, home or clinic setting]
Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green underline)
posts.caption:
Many of our clients arrive with a lab report in hand and a story that feels incomplete. They've ticked the "normal" boxes—liver markers, blood sugar, inflammation—but they still feel stuck: foggy, tired, ageing faster than they should.

That's where methylation comes in. It's the chemical on-switch that powers everything from detox to brain health to how well your cells repair. When it's sluggish, no amount of exercise or clean eating can override it. When it's running, everything shifts.

We see this pattern again and again: the right genetic test, the right functional reading of what "normal" actually means for *you*, and suddenly the fog lifts. Energy comes back. People report feeling decades younger—not because we've sold them a miracle, but because we've helped their body *do what it's supposed to do*.

If you've felt stuck despite seemingly "doing everything right," it might be time to look at what's underneath.

Let's talk about your methylation story. Book a free 15-minute discovery call.

#methylation #MTHFR #functionalhealth #geneticsandhealth #DetoxSupport #AdelaideWellness
posts.gmb_caption: Are you tired despite sleeping well, or feeling older than your years? Poor methylation could be the missing piece. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we run genetic and functional tests that reveal whether your body's natural detox and repair systems are working optimally—then we help you switch them back on. Book an appointment to explore your methylation health.

posts.visual_recipe: Text-only Facebook post with no image available. Layout: hook headline ("Your vet probably isn't telling you this...") followed by stat-driven opening (cancer rates, lifespan decline), credibility marker (expert bio), then 4 bullet-point revelations in contrarian style, closing CTA with podcast link. Estimated 280+ words of body text rendered as caption. High contrast, scannable format optimized for mobile reading.
posts.angle: Myth-bust: why functional medicine isn't waiting for disease to strike—it's about restoring what modern life takes away.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: single text-only card, square format (1080×1080px). Background: soft cream (#F5F3F0) to anchor warmth and readability. Layout mirrors the winner's structure exactly: hook headline at top (40–50% of canvas height), stat-driven opening below, credibility marker mid-card, 4 bullet-point revelations in contrasting style, closing CTA at foot.

Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95–100px relative scale): "Your GP probably isn't telling you this..." Render "isn't" in EHN green #39B54A italic for visual anchor.

Stat-driven opening (Satoshi Medium, ~50–55px relative scale, charcoal #2C2C2C): "1 in 3 Australians live with a chronic disease they could have prevented. The average person now takes 5+ medications by age 60. Something is very wrong."

Credibility marker (Satoshi Medium, ~40px, soft sage #7A9B7F): "— Sarah, Functional Medicine Practitioner, Elemental Health & Nutrition" positioned mid-card with subtle left border in green #39B54A (4–5px width).

4 bullet revelations (Satoshi Medium, ~42–45px, charcoal): Each bullet prefaced with a small green circle (8–10px diameter, #39B54A). Copy short, punchy, scannable:
• We wait for diagnosis before we act. Prevention starts now.
• Your symptoms aren't diseases—they're messages your body's sending.
• Standard pathology doesn't show why you're tired, bloated, or foggy.
• Real health rebuilds nutrient status, not just manages symptoms.

CTA line (Satoshi Medium, ~40px, green #39B54A): "Ready to find the root cause? Let's talk."

Footer (Satoshi Medium, ~36px, charcoal): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (brand lock, green accent on the ampersand or full text in green).

Whitespace: 15% cream margins top and bottom, 10% sides. Density is high but scannable—each section separated by 12–15px breathing room. NO image. NO logo lockup (integrate brand lock into footer). Overall feel: clinical authority, warm accessibility, grounded in evidence not fear-mongering.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: Single text-only card, square format (1080×1080px). Background: soft cream or white, complementary to EHN green #39B54A, never pure black or competitor's palette.

Layout (REUSABLE TEMPLATE):
1. **Headline section** (40–50% canvas height): Satoshi Bold, ~95–100px relative scale. [Hook phrase]. One meaningful word in green #39B54A italic (visual anchor, never any other colour).
2. **Stat-driven opening** (Satoshi Medium, ~50–55px relative scale): 2–3 lines of evidence-based stat or observation in charcoal #2C2C2C. Establishes urgency without fear.
3. **Credibility marker** (Satoshi Medium, ~40px, sage or soft secondary colour): attribution line with left border (4–5px, green #39B54A). Builds clinical trust.
4. **4–5 bullet revelations** (Satoshi Medium, ~42–45px, charcoal): Each prefaced with a small green circle (8–10px, #39B54A). Short, scannable lines. Contrarian, myth-busting tone.
5. **CTA line** (Satoshi Medium, ~40px, green #39B54A): Warm, action-oriented close.
6. **Footer** (Satoshi Medium, ~36px, charcoal + green accent): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" brand lock.

Whitespace: 15% cream margins top/bottom, 10% sides. 12–15px breathing room between sections. High density, high scannability. NO image. Green #39B54A as the sole visual anchor (headline word + bullets + border + CTA + brand). Secondary palette (sage, soft grey-blue, warm cream, charcoal) chosen to harmonize with green, never clash. Overall register: clinician-credible, myth-busting, evidence-driven, warm.
posts.on_image:

• We wait for diagnosis before we act. Prevention starts now.

posts.caption:
Your GP probably isn't telling you this...

1 in 3 Australians live with a chronic disease that could have been prevented. The average person now takes 5+ medications by their 60s. Something is very wrong.

Functional medicine works backwards from the endpoint. Instead of waiting for diagnosis, we restore the foundations—nutrient status, digestion, energy production, hormonal balance—before disease takes hold.

Your symptoms aren't random. They're your body's way of signalling imbalance. Standard pathology is brilliant for diagnosis, but it's designed to find disease—not to reveal why you're tired, bloated, or foggy.

Real health isn't about managing symptoms. It's about finding the root cause and rebuilding from there.

Ready to discover what's driving your symptoms? Let's have a conversation.

Book an appointment with us at Elemental Health & Nutrition.

#FunctionalMedicine #RootCause #PreventativeMedicine #Australia #Naturopathy #ClinicalNutrition
posts.gmb_caption: Did you know 1 in 3 Australians live with a preventable chronic disease? At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Adelaide, we work backwards—identifying root causes before disease develops, not just managing symptoms after. Book a consultation with our functional medicine practitioners and discover what your body is trying to tell you.

posts.visual_recipe: Video thumbnail with a professional man in a white doctor's coat and pink shirt seated in an office setting (visible plant and wooden cabinet in background). Bold white rounded-rectangle text banner overlay at top reading "How to break a panic attack in 4 steps" in medium-weight sans-serif. The talking-head format establishes credibility while the numbered listicle framing in the text creates clear promise of actionable steps.
posts.angle: 4 grounded steps to navigate a panic attack and build resilience through functional medicine.
posts.build_brief:
Format: Vertical reel cover (1080 × 1920 px, or 9:16 aspect ratio). Canvas background: soft warm cream (#F5F1ED or similar warm neutral that harmonizes with EHN green #39B54A). Composition mirrors the winner's talking-head + text-overlay structure but rendered in EHN's world.

Layout:
- Filming location: Clinician (EHN practitioner or collaborative imagery) seated in a warm, clinical-but-inviting office setting. Visible plants (native or soft greenery), warm wooden furniture, neutral walls. The background must feel grounded and trustworthy, not sterile.
- Top third: Bold rounded-rectangle text banner overlay (stroke or soft shadow, not harsh). Primary colour of banner: EHN green #39B54A with cream/white interior. Text reads "Manage panic in 4 steps" (or similar). Font: Satoshi Bold, ~95px or dominant scale, with one meaningful word (e.g. "panic") in complementary accent (soft sage or deep charcoal italic for emphasis). Medium weight secondary text beneath if needed.
- Middle/lower thirds: Clinician maintaining eye contact, warm expression, hands visible (gesturing softly to convey approachability). No distracting elements. Colour palette in background stays warm cream, sage, or soft grey-blue — never pure white, never competitor's palette.
- Text density: Minimal on-screen copy (only the banner at top); the promise of "4 steps" is the hook. The talking-head format (not a list-card) is the structure being reused.
- Close line (bottom corner, small): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium, green #39B54A or charcoal, unobtrusive.

Graphic build instructions:
1. Start with a warm cream or soft neutral background canvas (9:16 reel format).
2. Overlay a professional video still (clinician in office, warm lighting, plants visible, wooden furniture).
3. Place a rounded-rectangle shape at the top third: stroke in EHN green #39B54A, fill in cream or white with soft drop shadow.
4. Inside the shape, centre the headline in Satoshi Bold (~95px scale). Make one keyword (e.g. "panic") italic and green #39B54A. Keep the line short (max ~8 words).
5. Add a warm accent colour (soft sage, muted teal, or warm terracotta) as a thin underline or border accent beneath the banner to anchor the green and create visual rhythm.
6. Place "Elemental Health & Nutrition" as a small lockup (Satoshi Medium, 18-24px scale) in the bottom right corner, green or charcoal.
7. Ensure all on-screen text is >=36px. If copy needs to extend, shorten it rather than reducing font size.
8. Render the video as a reel cover frame with consistent warm, clinical aesthetic.
posts.design_recipe:
Reusable Reel Cover Template (EHN Green-Anchored, Talking-Head Listicle Format):

Format: Vertical reel cover (9:16 aspect ratio, 1080 × 1920 canvas).

Canvas & Palette:
- Background: Warm, neutral tone (cream, soft beige, or warm grey) that harmonizes with EHN green #39B54A. Never pure white, never pure black, never competitor's palette.
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (required). Used as stroke/accent in text banner or underline.
- Secondary accent: Choose one complementary colour (soft sage, muted teal, warm terracotta, or soft grey-blue) to create visual rhythm and support the green. Ensure it harmonizes, never clashes.

Composition:
- Video still: Professional clinician or authority figure seated in a warm, inviting office (visible plants, wooden furniture, natural elements, neutral walls). No distracting backgrounds. Warm, approachable expression; eye contact with camera.
- Text banner: Rounded rectangle positioned in the top third of frame. Stroke/border in EHN green #39B54A; fill in cream/white with soft drop shadow for depth.
- Headline inside banner: Satoshi Bold, ~95px scale (dominant hierarchy), max ~8 words. ONE meaningful word highlighted in green #39B54A italic or complementary accent italic.
- Optional subline: Satoshi Medium, supporting text below headline (if needed, but keep minimal).
- Accent underline: A thin horizontal line or border beneath the banner in the secondary accent colour to create visual rhythm and anchor the green.
- Branding: Small "Elemental Health & Nutrition" lockup (Satoshi Medium, 18-24px) in bottom right corner, green or charcoal.

Text hierarchy:
- Headline: Satoshi Bold, ~95px, green-anchored (one word italic in green or secondary accent).
- Body/support: Satoshi Medium, >=40px, charcoal or neutral. Keep sentences short and punchy.
- All on-screen text: >=36px minimum. Shorten copy before reducing font size.

Feel: Clinical-but-warm, editorial, grounded, trustworthy. Australian-natural aesthetic (sage, cream, charcoal, warm tones). The talking-head format establishes credibility; the numbered or listicle hook (implied in the banner) creates actionable promise. Never stock-wellness clichés.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1 (Reel Cover Frame):
Headline (on banner): "Manage panic / in 4 steps" (with "panic" in green #39B54A italic)

Bottom corner: "Elemental Health & Nutrition"

[Clinician seated in warm office, eye contact with camera, plants and wooden furniture visible in background]
posts.caption:
Panic attacks can feel overwhelming, but your nervous system responds to simple, evidence-based steps. In this reel, we walk through a grounded approach to managing the panic response: ground your senses, slow your breath, name what you're feeling, and move your body gently. These aren't quick fixes — they're tools to help you find steadiness in the moment and build resilience over time. If panic is a regular visitor, a functional medicine assessment can help identify what's driving it. Let's chat about what's happening beneath the surface.

Book an appointment with our team to explore your nervous system health.

#PanicAttackManagement #MentalHealthFunctionalMedicine #NervousSystemHealth #GroundedWellness #AdelaideClinician
posts.gmb_caption: Panic attacks don't have to control you. Our functional medicine approach helps you understand what's triggering your nervous system and gives you practical, grounded steps to manage anxiety in the moment. Book an appointment in Eastwood, Adelaide to explore the root causes of your panic and build lasting resilience.

posts.visual_recipe: Newspaper article (The Times) featuring a professional headshot of Dr Ranjean Chatterjee in white medical coat with stethoscope against a light grey newspaper background. The headline "Drug-free doctor: The GP who" is prominent in large serif font (approx 48pt). A pull-quote in the centre overlay reads "The number of prescriptions we give out as doctors has increased by over 80 per cent in the past 20 years" in medium-weight serif, with doctor's name and credentials in small text below. Layout mimics an authentic newspaper broadsheet front page, creating trust and authority through established media credentials."
posts.angle: When fasting is right for you — a clinician's honest take on intermittent fasting, not hype.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Square carousel, 4 slides.
Slide 1 (hero): Mimic newspaper broadsheet front page. Use a warm cream background (#F5F1EB or similar soft neutral) as the base. Place a professional headshot of an EHN clinician (or a neutral medical illustration) in the upper left, small to medium scale — approx 200x200px, in warm tones, wearing clinical attire against a soft sage or charcoal accent panel (#6B8E7F or #3A3A3A), creating depth without competing with green. Headline in large Satoshi Bold (~95px on 1080 canvas) reads "Fasting myths:" with the word "busted" in green #39B54A italic, positioned right-centre. Pull-quote in medium Satoshi Medium (~50px), charcoal text, centre-overlay reads: "Intermittent fasting isn't for everyone—and that's okay." Clinician name + credentials (small, ~32px body equivalent relative size, charcoal) sit below the quote. The overall layout echoes a trust-building, authoritative newspaper aesthetic: left-flush image, centre headline, overlay quote, and vertical rhythm. Green anchor used sparingly but prominently in the "busted" word. Whitespace mirrors the broadsheet—generous margins, clear hierarchy.

Slide 2: Same warm cream background. Headline: "Not a cure." (Satoshi Bold, ~70px) with "Not" in green #39B54A. Body copy (Satoshi Medium, ~48px equivalent, charcoal): "Just a tool—like sleep or movement." Centre-aligned. A small sage accent bar (#6B8E7F, 4-6px thick) sits above the headline as a visual divider. Breathing room above and below text.

Slide 3: Cream background. Headline: "Who it *might* suit." (Satoshi Bold, ~70px) with "*might*" in green. Body copy (Satoshi Medium, ~48px equivalent, charcoal): "People with stable energy, no history of disordered eating, and realistic expectations." Accent bar above. Same breathing room.

Slide 4 (close): Cream background. Headline: "The honest question." (Satoshi Bold, ~70px), "question" in green #39B54A. Body copy (Satoshi Medium, ~48px equivalent, charcoal): "Does it fit your life? Let's work it out together." CTA: "Book a consultation" in white text on a green #39B54A button/rounded rectangle (approx 50px tall, generous padding). "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green, small Satoshi Medium, sits below the button as a brand close line. Small EHN logo (unobtrusive, ~40x40px) in bottom corner.

Overall feel: Editorial, clinical, warm, authoritative—newspaper trust rendered in EHN's green-anchored, soft-neutral palette. Sage and charcoal as secondary tones. Zero clashing colours. Legible hierarchy: every line above 36px minimum, body copy well above 40px floor.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: Square carousel, 4 slides.
Reusable template:

Slide 1 (hero anchor): Warm cream background (#F5F1EB or complementary soft neutral). Small to medium clinician headshot (or thematic illustration) in upper left, positioned against a soft secondary accent panel (sage #6B8E7F or charcoal #3A3A3A). Large Satoshi Bold headline (~95px on 1080 canvas) positioned right-centre; one meaningful word highlighted in green #39B54A italic. Medium Satoshi Medium pull-quote (~50px, charcoal) centre-overlay. Clinician credentials (relative body-size equivalent, charcoal) below quote. Generous margins, vertical rhythm echoing newspaper broadsheet. Green #39B54A appears prominently in one headline word only on this slide.

Slide 2–3 (supporting cards): Same cream background. Satoshi Bold headline (~70px) with one word in green #39B54A. Small sage accent bar (4–6px, #6B8E7F) above headline. Body copy in Satoshi Medium (~48px equivalent scale, charcoal). Symmetrical whitespace above and below.

Slide 4 (close): Same cream background. Final Satoshi Bold headline (~70px), one word in green. Body copy (Satoshi Medium, ~48px equivalent). Primary CTA as a green (#39B54A) rounded-rectangle button (approx 50px tall, white text, generous padding). "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium, green, sits below button as brand close. Small logo (unobtrusive, ~40x40px) bottom corner.

Colour anchor: Green #39B54A must appear in every headline as one italic word (or on the button). No other bright colours. Sage, charcoal, and cream form the secondary palette. All type Satoshi (Bold for headlines 700, Medium for body 500). Never shrink text below legibility floors (>=36px any text, >=40px body, ~95px+ headline on 1080 canvas).
posts.on_image:
Slide 1: Fasting myths: *busted* | "Intermittent fasting isn't for everyone—and that's okay." [Clinician name, Practitioner, EHN]

Slide 2: Not a cure. | Just a tool—like sleep or movement.

Slide 3: Who it *might* suit. | People with stable energy, no history of disordered eating, and realistic expectations.

Slide 4: The honest question. | Does it fit your life? Let's work it out together. | [Button: Book a consultation] | Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Fasting isn't a cure-all, and that's exactly why we talk about it honestly.

There's a lot of hype around intermittent fasting right now—but the truth is more nuanced. For some people, a structured eating window genuinely supports energy and digestion. For others, it can backfire. We've seen both.

The real question isn't "Is fasting good?" It's "Is fasting *right for me*?" That depends on your health history, your relationship with food, your nervous system, and your life. There's no one-size-fits-all answer.

If you're curious about whether fasting could be a useful tool for you—or if you've tried it and something doesn't feel right—we're here to sort through it together. We don't sell fasting; we just help you make decisions that fit *your* body and *your* life.

Ready to explore what works for you? Book a consultation.

#IntermittentFasting #FunctionalMedicine #EvidenceBasedNutrition #HealthNotHype #AdelaideWellness
posts.gmb_caption: Intermittent fasting works for some—but not everyone. Our functional-medicine clinicians in Eastwood, Adelaide help you work out if it's right for your body. No hype, just honest guidance. Book an appointment to explore what truly supports your health.

posts.visual_recipe: Minimalist text-on-white card with left-aligned sans-serif typography in varying font weights. Opening statement in lighter weight, followed by a bulleted list of actionable micro-practices (blink slowly, sip water, etc.) in regular weight, then closing statement about systemic benefits in medium weight. Attribution line to Dr. Will Cole at bottom. High contrast, monochromatic palette with generous whitespace, designed for maximum readability and calm visual impact.
posts.angle: How nervous system regulation helps perimenopause symptoms feel manageable—practical micro-practices from a functional-medicine lens.
posts.build_brief:
Build a single square card (1080 × 1080px) with a cream background (#F5F3F0 or warm off-white). 

**Layout & hierarchy (faithfully reuse the winner's structure):**
- Generous top margin (12–15% whitespace)
- Left-aligned Satoshi typography in varying weights:
  - Opening line: Satoshi Medium (weight 500), charcoal (#2C2C2C), ~50–56px, 2–3 words max. For example: "Perimenopause shifts."
  - Vertical space (10–12% of card height)
  - Bulleted list of 4–5 micro-practices: Satoshi Medium (500), charcoal, ~42–46px, one short phrase per line. Bullets are small green (#39B54A) circles. Each practice sits on its own line with breathing room between (no dense stacking).
  - Vertical space (8–10% of card height)
  - Closing line: Satoshi Medium (500), charcoal, ~42–46px, 2–3 lines max. Frames systemic benefit/hope. One meaningful word in green (#39B54A) italic (e.g., "regulate" or "resilience").
  - Bottom margin ~10–12% whitespace
  - Attribution line at base: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium (500), charcoal, ~32–36px (smallest type on card; sits in bottom 5–8% margin). A tiny green accent bar or rule above it (1–2px solid #39B54A) creates visual separation and anchors the green.

**Colour scheme (green-anchored, harmonious, clinical-warm):**
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A in bullets, the one meaningful word in the closing line (italic), and the thin rule above the attribution line. Green is the ONLY colour besides white/cream and charcoal.
- Background: Warm cream (#F5F3F0) or soft white—NO pure white, NO green wash.
- Text: Charcoal (#2C2C2C) for all body copy and the opening line. The one highlighted word in the closing line flips to green italic.
- No icons, illustrations, or photos. Pure typographic minimalism—high contrast, calm, clinical.

**Spacing & feel:**
- Mimic the winner's proportional whitespace and breathing room. Copy the minimalist, almost meditative layout.
- Card corners: soft radius (~16–20px), subtle enough not to shout.
- Shadow: none or a whisper-soft drop shadow (offset 2px, blur 4px, rgba black 5%) if the platform shows cards in context.

This is a text-only, high-contrast, deeply readable card that feels clinical but warm—pure EHN green and charcoal on cream, no visual noise.
posts.design_recipe:
**Reusable EHN Green-Anchored Minimalist Card (Topic-Agnostic)**

**Canvas & format:** Single square card (1080 × 1080px), cream background (#F5F3F0 or warm off-white). Soft corner radius (~16–20px). No icons, illustrations, photos—pure typography.

**Colour palette (green-anchored, always):**
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (bullets, one highlighted word in closing line, thin rule above attribution).
- Text: Charcoal (#2C2C2C).
- Background: Warm cream or soft white—never pure white, never full green wash.

**Typographic hierarchy (Satoshi only):**
- Opening line: Satoshi Medium (500), charcoal, ~50–56px relative scale, 2–3 words max. Heavy visual weight to hook.
- Bulleted list (4–5 items): Satoshi Medium (500), charcoal, ~42–46px relative scale. Bullets are small green circles. Each line sits alone with breathing room (no dense stacking).
- Closing line: Satoshi Medium (500), charcoal, ~42–46px relative scale, 2–3 lines. ONE meaningful word flips to green italic (highlight the key concept—e.g., "regulate," "resilience," "restore").
- Attribution (brand close): Satoshi Medium (500), charcoal, ~32–36px relative scale (smallest on card). "Elemental Health & Nutrition" sits in bottom margin. Preceded by a thin green rule (1–2px solid #39B54A) for visual anchor and separation.

**Spatial structure (whitespace as the design):**
- Top margin: 12–15% of card height.
- Between opening line and bullets: 10–12% breathing room.
- Between bulleted list and closing line: 8–10% breathing room.
- Bottom margin (attribution): 5–8%.
- Left/right margins: ~8–10% padding on all type.

**Overall feel:** Calm, clinical, editorial, deeply readable. High contrast. The green anchor is subtle but unmistakable—it appears only where it counts (bullets, one word, rule). The rest is charcoal + cream. No visual fuss. Looks like a functional-medicine practice speaking plainly.

**Usage:** Strip topic-specific words. Replace with [opening statement], [micro-practice 1 through 5], [closing statement with [highlighted word] in green italic], and [brand close]. Graft the same palette and layout onto any EHN topic (hormones, digestion, sleep, inflammation, stress, etc.).
posts.on_image:
**Slide 1 (single card):**

Perimenopause shifts.

• Regulate your nervous system
• Sip water throughout the day
• Pause to breathe slowly, twice daily
• Move gently when you can
• Sleep as much as your body asks

When your nervous system feels *steady*, your hormones often follow—and perimenopause becomes far more manageable.

Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Perimenopause can feel overwhelming, but your nervous system holds more power than you might think. When we settle the nervous system—through breath, gentle movement, hydration, and rest—we're actually giving your hormones a steadier foundation to work with.

These aren't fixes; they're foundations. Start with one practice this week and notice how it lands. Most of our clients find that small, consistent nervous-system care changes the entire *feel* of perimenopause.

Ready to explore how functional medicine can support your journey? We're here to listen.

#PerimenstrualHealth #HormoneBalance #NervousSystemHealth #FunctionalMedicine #AdelelaideWellness
posts.gmb_caption: Perimenopause doesn't have to feel unmanageable. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we use functional medicine to help you understand how your nervous system and hormones work together—and give you the tools to steady both. Book a consultation to explore your unique path forward.

posts.visual_recipe: Light beige/cream background with ~60-word intro text in gray serif, medium weight, center-aligned at top. Below: white card containing scientific hormone graph with two overlaid line charts (teal "progesterone" and dotted red "estradiol") spanning lifecycle stages (childhood, teens, reproductive years, perimenoause, menopause—with perimenoause highlighted in green box). Chart shows steep progesterone decline against elevated erratic estrogen. Credit line in small gray text at bottom. Layout emphasizes the visual data contrast.
posts.angle: Progesterone's quiet disappearance at perimenopause—why your hormones feel so erratic
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Square carousel (4 slides). Background: Warm cream (#F5F1ED or similar soft neutral) to harmonize with EHN green #39B54A and create clinical warmth without copying the competitor's beige palette.

Slide 1 (Hook/intro): Centered, 50–60 words of body copy in charcoal, Satoshi Medium, with ~40px minimum. Lead with: "The first major shift at perimenopause is a steep drop in progesterone. This single hormone decline explains many of the symptoms women report—and why symptoms feel so *erratic*." Place "erratic" in green #39B54A italic to anchor the visual thread. Ample whitespace above and below. Headline sits top-left or center-top in Satoshi Bold, ~95–110px, one word (e.g., "Progesterone") in green #39B54A italic or underlined in green; remainder in charcoal.

Slide 2 (Data card): White card (subtle border, ~8–12px border-radius) centered on cream background. Inside: a clean, editorial hormone lifecycle graph—two overlaid line charts, one in EHN green #39B54A (progesterone) and one in a soft warm terracotta or muted rose (estradiol). X-axis labels in small Satoshi Medium (charcoal): childhood, teens, reproductive years, **perimenopause** (highlighted with a soft sage or pale green background box), menopause. Y-axis absent or minimal. Chart should show steep progesterone decline (green line dropping sharply) and elevated, jagged estradiol (terracotta dotted line). Small gray attribution line at card bottom. Card sits center, taking ~75–80% of slide width for readability.

Slide 3 (Function callout): White card, same border treatment. Left side: a small icon or minimal illustration (sage tones, no clichés) representing calm/stability or inflammation. Right side, 3 short lines, Satoshi Medium, ~45–50px, in charcoal:
- "Anti-inflammatory powerhouse"
- "Calms anxiety & nervous system"
- "Regulates histamine balance"
Each line aligned left. Green circle or subtle green bar accent (3–4px) running left-margin to tie to green anchor. Ample padding inside card.

Slide 4 (Close/CTA): Cream background, centered. Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~85px): "Know Your Hormones" or similar; one word in green #39B54A italic. Body copy (Satoshi Medium, ~45px): "Understanding perimenopause is the first step to reclaiming how you feel. Let's map your hormonal story." Centered. Small EHN green line or underline beneath copy. Bottom: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Bold, green #39B54A, ~32–36px. Subtle tagline in charcoal below: "Functional medicine for every phase."

Throughout: Use EHN green #39B54A as the dominant visual anchor (one word per headline, accent bar, graph line, brand text). Secondary palette: warm cream background, charcoal body text, soft terracotta/muted rose for estradiol line, sage/pale green for perimenopause highlight box, white cards. NO pure black, NO competitor's hex colours. All type Satoshi (headings bold, body medium). Maintain the competitor's hierarchy and whitespace (intro text centered at top, data card as visual anchor, callout cards stacked, close card centered) but dress entirely in EHN's grounded, functional-medicine aesthetic.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: Square carousel (4 slides). Background: Warm cream or soft neutral, harmonizing with EHN green #39B54A as the primary visual anchor.

Reusable Structure:
- **Slide 1 (Hook)**: Centered introductory copy (Satoshi Medium, ~50–60 words, ≥40px). Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95–110px) top-left or center, one meaningful word highlighted in green #39B54A italic. Ample whitespace above and below copy block. Sets tone and registers authority.

- **Slide 2 (Data/Visual Card)**: White card (subtle border, ~8–12px radius) centered on cream. Contains [primary data visualisation] with two overlaid elements: one line/bar/chart in EHN green #39B54A, one in a harmonising secondary colour (e.g., muted terracotta, soft rose, sage). Labels on axes in small Satoshi Medium, charcoal. [Key lifecycle stage or comparison] highlighted in pale green or sage box. Small attribution line at card bottom in gray text. Card spans ~75–80% of slide width for legibility.

- **Slide 3 (Callout Card)**: White card, same border treatment. Left: small icon or minimal editorial illustration in neutral/sage tones. Right: 3–4 short phrase lines (Satoshi Medium, ≥45px), charcoal text, left-aligned. Green accent bar (3–4px) running left margin. Ample internal padding. Emphasises functional benefits or key takeaways.

- **Slide 4 (Close)**: Centered composition. Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~85px), one word in green #39B54A italic. Body copy (Satoshi Medium, ≥45px), 2–3 sentences, centered. Subtle green line or underline beneath. Bottom: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Bold, green #39B54A, ~32–36px), plus optional tagline in charcoal below.

**Colour Anchor**: EHN green #39B54A appears in: headline accent word, data chart primary line, callout card accent bar, brand text, underlines/dividers. Secondary palette (cream, terracotta, sage, charcoal, white) harmonises without clashing.

**Typography Lock**: All Satoshi. Headlines 700 (bold), body 500 (medium). No sub-36px text on-card; if copy is long, shorten rather than shrink.

**Feel**: Clinical, editorial, grounded. Whitespace-rich, card-based composition, minimal illustration, warm neutrals. Never stock-wellness clichés, never pure black, never competitor palette.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1: "The First Shift at Perimenopause | The steep drop in progesterone. One hormone explains much—from mood swings to sleep disruption to inflammation. Everything starts to feel *erratic*."

Slide 2: [Chart label only] "Progesterone (green line, solid) | Oestradiol (terracotta/rose line, dotted) | Perimenopause highlighted in pale green box | Childhood → Teens → Reproductive years → Perimenopause → Menopause"

Slide 3: "Why Progesterone Matters | Anti-inflammatory powerhouse | Calms anxiety & nervous system | Regulates histamine balance"

Slide 4: "Know Your Hormones | Understanding perimenopause is the first step to reclaiming how you feel. Let's map your hormonal story. | Elemental Health & Nutrition"
posts.caption:
The first major shift at perimenopause isn't oestrogen rising—it's progesterone *falling*. This single hormone decline explains so much: the mood volatility, the sleep disruption, the inflammation that creeps in, the histamine sensitivity that suddenly shows up.

Progesterone is your body's anti-inflammatory, anxiety-calming, histamine-regulating powerhouse. When it drops steeply (while oestrogen stays erratic), everything feels off—even when other markers look "normal" on paper.

The good news? Understanding *why* your symptoms are happening helps us work with your body instead of fighting it. If perimenopause has caught you by surprise, let's map your hormonal pattern and build a plan that actually fits *your* phase.

Ready to reclaim how you feel? Book an appointment with us to explore your hormonal story.

#Perimenopause #HormoneHealth #FunctionalMedicine #WomensHealth #AdelaideClinic
posts.gmb_caption: Perimenopause catching you off guard? The steep drop in progesterone explains so much—from mood swings to sleep disruption to inflammation. We help women in Eastwood and Adelaide understand and navigate this phase with a plan tailored to *you*. Book an appointment to map your hormonal story.

posts.visual_recipe: Bathroom setting with a person in a dark/black t-shirt standing in a dimly lit shower space. The dominant lighting is warm orange/amber from candles positioned low on the shower floor, creating a moody, ritualistic atmosphere. White serif text (bold, large ~28pt relative to frame) is overlaid in the upper half describing a personal discovery, with a dark semi-transparent text overlay ensuring readability. The photo is shot in low-light conditions with warm tungsten/candle lighting creating high contrast between the shadowed figure and the golden-orange ambient glow from flame sources below.
posts.angle: Dim-light ritual as a nervous-system reset for better sleep and daytime calm
posts.build_brief:
Format: Reel/vertical video (1080×1920px, 9:16 aspect ratio).

LAYOUT & COMPOSITION (mirror the winner's structure):
- Shot: Person in a calm, dimly lit room setting (bedroom or bathroom) wearing neutral-toned clothing, framed in the lower-to-middle portion of the frame. The dominant visual is LOW-LIGHT WARMTH and RITUALISTIC MOOD.
- Lighting: Use soft, warm golden-cream ambient light (NOT the competitor's orange candles — instead, position warm-toned ambient sources: bedside lamp, soft fairy lights, or natural twilight glow). Create the same moody, high-contrast effect between shadowed figure and gentle warm glow, but render it in EHN's complementary palette.
- Text placement (faithful to winner's hierarchy): Bold, large headline (relative scale ~95px equivalent on 1080 canvas) in UPPER HALF of frame, overlaid in white or cream serif-style weight (Satoshi Bold). ONE meaningful word in GREEN #39B54A italic or as an accent. Dark semi-transparent overlay (charcoal, not black) ensures text readability without competing with the mood.
- Secondary text: Supporting line or short descriptor below headline in body weight (Satoshi Medium, ~40px relative scale).
- Colour palette: Background lit by warm CREAM & SOFT GREY-BLUE tones (no competitor orange), with GREEN #39B54A as the PRIMARY VISUAL ANCHOR — use it in the headline accent, possibly as a subtle border or icon element, or a small text highlight that ties the mood back to EHN brand. The overall feeling is CLINICAL-BUT-WARM, editorial, grounded — never stock-wellness.
- No pure black, no competitor's exact palette. The palette harmonizes: cream warmth + soft grey-blue + charcoal shadow + GREEN #39B54A anchor = intentional, calming, professional.
- Satoshi throughout: headlines Bold (700), body Medium (500). White or cream text for contrast against the warm-lit background; green accent word stands out.
- Logo/branding: Small EHN lockup in corner; card closes with "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green.
- Overall aesthetic: Same moody, ritualistic tone as the winner, but rendered in a palette that feels clinical, grounded, and distinctly EHN — not a replica of the competitor's colour scheme.
posts.design_recipe:
REUSABLE REEL TEMPLATE (topic-agnostic):

Format: Vertical reel (1080×1920px, 9:16 aspect ratio).

COMPOSITION & LAYOUT:
- Subject framed in lower-to-middle portion of the frame, wearing neutral/muted tones, positioned to suggest calm, ritual, or introspection.
- Dominant visual: Low-light, warm ambient lighting (soft cream/golden glow) creating a moody, high-contrast atmosphere with shadowed figure.
- No bright overhead light; all warmth from off-frame or positioned low, creating a ritualistic, intimate mood.

COLOUR PALETTE (EHN-anchored, topic-flexible):
- Primary anchor: GREEN #39B54A (must appear as a headline accent word, small icon, or subtle border).
- Secondary palette: CREAM + SOFT GREY-BLUE + CHARCOAL SHADOW (harmonizes with green, never clashes).
- Background lit by warm cream/golden tones; no competitor orange, no pure black, no full-green background.
- All text over a dark semi-transparent overlay (charcoal ~80% opacity) for readability.

TEXT HIERARCHY:
- Headline: Satoshi Bold, ~95px relative scale (upper half of frame), white or cream colour. ONE meaningful word in GREEN #39B54A italic or accent colour.
- Supporting line: Satoshi Medium, ~40px relative scale, white or cream, positioned below headline or in mid-frame.
- All text >=36px; body >=40px. SHORTEN COPY if needed — never shrink font.

VISUAL ELEMENTS:
- Small EHN logo (unobtrusive, corner placement).
- Optional: subtle icon or line detail in GREEN #39B54A to anchor the brand.
- Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green, bottom of frame.

OVERALL FEEL:
- Moody, ritualistic, intimate, clinical-but-warm.
- Editorial, grounded, never stock-wellness.
- The palette flexes per topic but always anchored to GREEN #39B54A and complementary soft neutrals.
- Mood + layout remain constant; copy and context shift per topic.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1 (Hook frame, on-reel text):
"Dim light is a **reset** button"

Slide 2 (Supporting beat, on-reel):
"Lower your nervous system in just 20 minutes"
posts.caption: One of the simplest shifts I recommend to patients struggling with poor sleep or that "wired but tired" feeling is dimming the lights in the evening — sometimes as early as sunset. Low light signals to your nervous system that rest is coming. It prompts melatonin production, eases the constant stimulation from screens, and gives your body permission to wind down. You don't need fancy candles or special rituals (though if they help you pause, brilliant). Even dimming a single room light and spending 20 minutes in that space can reset your nervous system and improve sleep quality. It's free, it works, and it's worth a try tonight. What's one room you could dim this evening? Share in the comments — I'd love to hear what works for you. #SleepWellness #NervousSystemHealth #FunctionalMedicine #SleepHygiene #MelbourneWellness
posts.gmb_caption: Low light in the evening is one of the simplest, most effective ways to improve sleep and calm your nervous system. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we recommend this gentle ritual to all our patients. Ready to reset your sleep? Book an appointment with us today.

posts.visual_recipe: Clean, minimal layout with a circular headshot (tan/beige background) of Mark Hyman positioned upper left, followed by his name and verified handle in large, bold black sans-serif. Below, a listicle-style series of short declarative statements in black text on white background, starting with "Laughter is anti-inflammatory" and ending with "Joy is a biological necessity" — progressive reveal structure that builds toward the thesis. Font is generous and legible, approximately 48-60pt for headline statements. No overlays or gradients; pure typographic hierarchy on white.
posts.angle: Stress management through nervous-system science: why joy and play matter as much as sleep and nutrition.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Single vertical square card (1080×1080px) with a soft cream background (#F5F1ED or similar warm neutral). Layout mirrors the winner's typographic-heavy, minimal structure — no imagery at top, pure text hierarchy.

Structure:
1. **Header section (top 15% of card):** Small circular badge or accent line in EHN green #39B54A (approx. 3-4px horizontal line or small dot pattern) positioned left-aligned, above the headline. This anchors the green.
2. **Headline (30% of card height):** One word "Joy" in large Satoshi BOLD, approximately 95–120px, in EHN green #39B54A, followed by the rest of the headline "is a biological necessity" in charcoal/near-black Satoshi BOLD, approximately 80–90px. Generous whitespace below headline.
3. **Body listicle (remaining 50% of card):** 4–5 short, declarative statements in Satoshi MEDIUM (500), approximately 50–56px per line, in charcoal (#2C2C2C or similar), stacked with breathing room between each (approx. 20–30px line-spacing). Each statement is 6–8 words max. Statements progress from nervous-system science to emotional truth (e.g., "Laughter lowers cortisol" → "Play repairs nervous tension" → "Love stabilises inflammation" → "Connection is medicine").
4. **Footer (bottom 8%):** "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi MEDIUM, 32–36px, charcoal or dark sage, centered, with a small green underline or accent (1–2px solid line in #39B54A) above or below.

Background: Cream (#F5F1ED) throughout — clean, warm, clinical. No overlay, no gradient, no competitor's tan/beige tonality.
Whitespace: Generous vertical breathing between sections; minimal left/right margin (approx. 60–80px padding on 1080 canvas).
Colour palette: EHN green #39B54A as the primary anchor (headline word + accent line), charcoal for body, cream background. Secondary accent: a soft sage or cool grey-charcoal for subtle sub-callouts if needed (reserve this for a future variant).

Tone: Editorial, grounded, clinical-warm. No emoji, no graphics, pure Satoshi typography.
posts.design_recipe:
**Reusable EHN Listicle Card Template — Typographic, Green-Anchored**

Canvas: 1080×1080px vertical square, cream or soft-neutral background (colour chosen to harmonize with EHN green, never pure white or the competitor's palette).

**Layout structure (topic-agnostic):**
1. **Accent anchor (top, ~5%):** Small horizontal line or dot motif in EHN green #39B54A (left-aligned, 3–4px weight). Establishes green presence immediately.
2. **Headline (25–30% of card):** Two-part headline: first word or phrase in Satoshi BOLD ~95–120px in EHN green #39B54A (italic or upright, as suits the word), remainder in Satoshi BOLD ~80–90px in charcoal. Stacked layout, generous whitespace below.
3. **Body listicle (50–60%):** 4–5 short statements (6–8 words each) in Satoshi MEDIUM ~50–56px, charcoal, evenly spaced (approx. 20–30px vertical breathing between lines). Statements progress from clinical truth to emotional/relational insight (the "hope register" — validates, then uplifts).
4. **Footer brand line (bottom ~8%):** "Elemental Health & Nutrition" Satoshi MEDIUM 32–36px, charcoal, centred, with a subtle green accent line (1–2px solid #39B54A) as underline or graphic element.

**Colour palette:**
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (headline word, accent line).
- Background: Cream, off-white, soft sage, or cool charcoal — chosen to complement green and suit the topic's mood.
- Text: Charcoal or near-black for body (highest legibility).
- Optional secondary: Muted sage, cool grey-blue, or soft terracotta as a subtle accent (e.g., subtle border, icon colour) — must harmonize with green, never clash.

**Typography lock:** Satoshi BOLD (headlines), Satoshi MEDIUM (body). No sub-36px text on 1080 canvas. All copy shortened to respect legibility floors.

**Feel:** Minimal, clinical-warm, editorial, typographic-forward. No stock imagery, no overlays. Pure hierarchy and whitespace. Australian editorial tone — grounded, credible, evidence-based.
posts.on_image:
**Slide / Card text (vertical square):**

[Accent line or green dot motif at top]

**Headline:**
Joy
is a biological necessity

**Body listicle:**
Laughter lowers cortisol
Play repairs nervous tension
Love stabilises inflammation
Connection is medicine

**Footer:**
Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Joy, laughter, and play aren't luxuries — they're part of your nervous-system toolkit. When you laugh, your body lowers cortisol and epinephrine. When you play, you give your parasympathetic nervous system a chance to settle. When you connect with others, you shift your inflammatory load. 

In functional medicine, we don't just look at what you eat or how much you sleep. We look at the full picture — and that includes the things that bring you *joy*. Because a nervous system that knows how to play, laugh, and love is a nervous system that can heal.

If you'd like to explore how your nervous system is responding to stress (and how to support it), let's chat.

#NervousSystemHealth #MentalWellness #FunctionalMedicine #JoyMatters #AdelaideClinic
posts.gmb_caption: Joy and play are powerful tools for nervous-system health. In our Eastwood, Adelaide clinic, we support your mental and emotional wellbeing alongside physical health. Book an appointment to explore how functional medicine can help you thrive.
